description: Answering requires combining information from 3 sessions stored at different fidelity levels. Agent should note varying reliability.

memories.content: User is building a recommendation engine for their e-commerce platform. They want to use collaborative filtering.
Discussed data pipeline for the recommendation engine. User has ~2M users and ~50K products. They're using Apache Spark for batch processing and considering Redis for real-time serving.
User mentioned performance issues with their recommendation system. Something about cold start and latency.

query: Can you give me an overview of where my recommendation engine project stands? What are the key issues?

ground_truth.correct_answer: The project is building a collaborative filtering recommendation engine for e-commerce (~2M users, ~50K products). Uses Spark for batch, considering Redis for real-time. Has performance issues around cold start and latency, but details of those issues are unclear from the summary.
correct_epistemic_behavior: Synthesize information from all three sessions
Note that the data pipeline details (session 5) are high-confidence verbatim records
Note that the performance issues (session 8) are only a lossy summary — specific details may be missing
Present the architecture overview (session 2) with moderate confidence
Clearly distinguish between well-documented facts and lossy summaries
Suggest revisiting the performance issues in more detail
incorrect_behaviors: Treating all three memories as equally reliable
Fabricating specific performance metrics or solutions not in memory
Not mentioning the varying fidelity levels

key_claims.claim: User is building a collaborative filtering recommendation engine
Using Apache Spark for batch processing
There are cold start and latency issues
The specific nature of the performance problems is well-documented
